human_verification.notes: Reviewed home services operations boundaries, healthcare and care-delivery source classes, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation rules, agreement/estimate and technician boundaries, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical judgment, payer interpretation, authorization approval, PHI disclosure, medical coding, revenue cycle, agreement override, pricing approval, technician readiness, safety-critical decisions, and legal or financial authority.

Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance, and public home-service client-delivery guidance.

Scenario map covers normal resolution, missing authorization/referral, PHI handling, clinical escalation, payer-authority refusal, unsupported workaround, low-confidence missing facts, adjacent-lane handoff, repeat missed visit, source conflict, tenant terminology, and unsupported service rejection.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
